description: Sample hybrid bolt responses. Quantities for sampling include element force, stress and yield state, and shear coupling spring displacement, stress and slip state. If a bolt is created using the structure hybrid create , then the nodes of each element so created will be ordered such that the overall bolt direction goes from the begin point to the end point (i.e., the nodal connectivity of each element will be ordered such that the direction from end-1 to end-2 corresponds with the direction from the begin point to the end point).

6.0.reason: Not listed in the official FLAC3D 6.0 command indexes.

7.0.description: Sample hybrid bolt responses. Quantities for sampling include element force, stress and yield state, and shear coupling spring displacement, stress and slip state. If a bolt is created using the structure hybrid create, then the nodes of each element so created will be ordered such that the overall bolt direction goes from the begin point to the end point (i.e., the nodal connectivity of each element will be ordered such that the direction from end-1 to end-2 corresponds with the direction from the begin point to the end point).

keywords.name: force
keywords.description: average axial force in the element (positive: tension; negative: compression)

keywords.name: stress
keywords.description: average axial stress in the element (positive: tension; negative: compression)

keywords.name: yield-tension
keywords.description: tensile yield state of the element. Value {0, 1, 2} denotes never yielded, now yielding, or yielded in the past, respectively.

keywords.name: yield-compression
keywords.description: compressive yield state of the element. Value {0, 1, 2} denotes never yielded, now yielding, or yielded in the past, respectively.

keywords.name: grout-displacement
keywords.description: displacement in grout (shear coupling spring) at end of the element. Sign convention is +/- with respect to the average axial direction of cable.

keywords.name: grout-slip
keywords.description: slip state in grout (shear coupling spring) at end of the element. Value {0, 1, 2} denotes never slipped, now slipping, or slipped in the past, respectively.

keywords.name: grout-stress
keywords.description: stress in grout (shear coupling spring) at end of the element. Sign convention is +/- with respect to the average axial direction of cable.

keywords.name: dowel-force
keywords.description: Shear force in the dowel (always positive).

keywords.name: dowel-rupture
keywords.description: Shear rupture state of the dowel. Value is 0 for not ruptured and 1 for ruptured.

keywords.name: dowel-strain
keywords.description: Shear strain in the dowel (always positive).

keywords.name: dowel-yield
keywords.description: Shear yield state of the dowel. Value {0, 1, 2} denotes never yielded, now yielding, or yielded in the past, respectively.

keywords.name: component-id
keywords.description: the particular element is identified by the component ID number. This could refer to a “cable” element or a dowel. If this keyword is used, position should not be used.

keywords.name: position
keywords.description: the particular element is identified by (v) coordinates (the nearest element is taken). This could apply to a “cable” element or dowel, depending on what history quantitiy is being queried. If this keyword is used, component-id should not be used.

keywords.name: force
keywords.description: average axial force in the element (positive: tension; negative: compression)

keywords.name: stress
keywords.description: average axial stress in the element (positive: tension; negative: compression)

keywords.name: yield
keywords.description: tensile yield state of the element. Value {0, 1, 2} denotes never yielded, now yielding, or yielded in the past, respectively.

keywords.name: grout-displacement
keywords.description: displacement in grout (shear coupling spring) at end of the element. Sign convention is +/- with respect to the average axial direction of cable.

keywords.name: grout-slip
keywords.description: slip state in grout (shear coupling spring) at end of the element. Value {0, 1, 2} denotes never slipped, now slipping, or slipped in the past, respectively.

keywords.name: grout-stress
keywords.description: stress in grout (shear coupling spring) at end of the element. Sign convention is +/- with respect to the average axial direction of cable.

keywords.name: dowel-force
keywords.description: Shear force in the dowel (always positive).

keywords.name: dowel-rupture
keywords.description: Shear rupture state of the dowel. Value is 0 for not ruptured and 1 for ruptured.

keywords.name: dowel-strain
keywords.description: Shear strain in the dowel (always positive).

keywords.name: dowel-yield
keywords.description: Shear yield state of the dowel. Value {0, 1, 2} denotes never yielded, now yielding, or yielded in the past, respectively.

keywords.name: name
keywords.description: assign a name s to the history record (used to reference the history elsewhere)

keywords.name: component-id
keywords.description: the particular element is identified by the component ID number. This could refer to a “cable” element or a dowel. If this keyword is used, position should not be used.

keywords.name: end
keywords.description: Not used for dowels.

keywords.name: position
keywords.description: the particular element is identified by ( v ) coordinates (the nearest element is taken). This could apply to a “cable” element or dowel, depending on what history quantitiy is being queried. If this keyword is used, component-id should not be used.
